Why Kinectiv

A marketing-first partner for the way people find you now

We help organizations shape the way they look, feel, and interact, online and in the real world. We approach every web project from a marketing-first lens, which means design, functionality, and user experience are all built to support one thing: clarity, trust, and action for the people who matter to your business.

We are a full-service marketing agency and our specialty is identity and experience design, which increasingly means building sites that are not just visually right, but structurally ready for how people, and AI, actually find you. We use AI ourselves to move faster on research, structure, and iteration. Every decision about your brand and your site is still made by our team.

Our team is a mix of creative and technical people, and the chemistry between them is what produces the best result for each client. The core team oversees every project from start to finish, so the same standard holds whether the work is branding, print, or a full website build like this one.

We work with local businesses, regional companies, and non-profit organizations to build memorable identities and the experiences that carry them, whether online through a website, in the real world through print and advertising, or a blend of both.

Selected partners: The Fulton Theatre, Aaron’s Acres, A Week Away Foundation, Penn Cinema, Elizabethtown Public Library, Lancaster Mennonite School, The Hourglass Foundation, Eden Resort, and many more.
Our Vision

Our vision for the project

Our plan is simple to state: rebuild Wolf & Kline’s website so it works the way your renters and owners actually think. Single-family and multifamily paths split from the first click, every community carrying its own identity, and every visit trackable back to whether it became an application.

And built, from day one, to be found, not just by Google, but by the AI tools an increasing share of renters now ask directly. The outcome is a website that supports both sides of your business, renters finding a home and owners trusting their investment, and makes it easier for each to find what they need and take the next step.

Timeline: a typical build of this scope runs four to six months from Website Outline approval to launch.
Discovery

Learning, analyzing, and scoping

During our discovery conversation on July 2, 2026, we spent time understanding Wolf & Kline’s business: single-family and multifamily portfolios, resident and owner relationships, and more than 30 years of reputation across Lancaster and Lebanon County. We also heard clearly what is creating friction today:

  • A CMS so restrictive that a routine hero-image change nearly broke the site.
  • Mobile bounce rates running 60 to 70 percent, on a site where mobile is 65 to 75 percent of all traffic.
  • No visibility connecting ad spend to applications actually submitted.
  • An SEO approach built for a search era that is already ending.

We also heard what is already working in your favor: genuine FAQ content, real local specificity across dozens of communities, and three decades of reviews and reputation. The current site simply is not yet structured to make the most of it.

Completed

  • Initial discovery session held with Scott and the Wolf & Kline team (July 2, 2026).
  • Technical and AI-search (AEO) review of the current site conducted in advance, so the conversation started from real findings, not a generic checklist.

Ahead

  • Technical SEO/AEO and conversion-tracking audit (see AI Search & Data Strategy, below).
  • Website Outline and project timeline presented and agreed upon.
  • A dedicated project manager assigned to your project.
Project Approach

How we’ll build it

Once we understood what Wolf & Kline needs from a partner, someone proactive, technically capable, and thinking the way renters and AI search now do, we translated that into a plan for a site that works as hard as your team does. From there, our job is to concept and build an online presence that positions the business properly, serves renters and owners equally well, and gives your team room to grow.

The new Wolf & Kline website will be built through the creative and analytical collaboration of the Kinectiv and Wolf & Kline teams, and will deliver a completely reimagined experience aimed at intuitive resources, meaningful information, and positive interactions for every kind of visitor.

Two audiences, two clear paths

Wolf & Kline serves two real audiences with two different goals: renters searching for a home and owners trusting their investment. We will split those paths from the first click and build navigation and page structure so each audience finds what it needs immediately, without digging through content meant for the other.

Community identity that scales

Your apartment communities each deserve their own feel: individual logos, color schemes, and personality. Those assets already exist. The current WordPress setup cannot flex to carry them, which is a large part of what triggered this search. We will build a flexible community-page template so every property keeps its own identity without every page having to be hand-built from scratch. On the apartment side, we will also shift from individual unit pages to floor-plan pages, each with a photo, information, and a dropdown of available units and addresses, which meaningfully reduces the roughly 1,000 to 1,500 individual listing pages you manage today.

A clean, self-serve experience

The existing site gets in its own way. The new site will feature a clean design and a structure that guides users along clear pathways to what matters to them, with strong visuals paired with concise, clear copy. It will be easy to navigate, adapt well to mobile, meet accessibility standards, and connect users with the resources relevant to them. Just as important, your team will be able to make routine changes, phone numbers, photos, text, without waiting on us.

Built to be found

Across the board, we approach this site with the mindset of an experience that can actually be discovered, by search engines and by the AI tools renters increasingly ask directly. That is not an add-on at the end. It is woven into structure, content, and tracking from the start, which is what turns traffic into applications you can measure.

The Process

Website process overview

01

Project Kickoff

Upon signing an agreed-upon contract, we’ll introduce you to your project manager and begin the two-week onboarding period.

02

Discovery

We meet with you to gain a deeper understanding of who you are as an organization, your unique audience and goals, and to develop a concept for what the site needs to accomplish.

03

Strategy

Guided by the information gathered in discovery, we provide a wireframe built using our proprietary FlexSite sections and populated with placeholder content.

04

Content

An outline of each page is provided to prompt you and your team with the content you’d like to populate the site.

05

Design

We implement your brand elements to define the theme style and create a mock-up of your homepage and custom section(s).

06

Development

In this phase, we create the website, apply the styling defined by the designer, and build each custom section.

07

Launch

Our team optimizes the content you provide and populates the site for launch. After internal and client review, your site is soft launched (a launch that is not announced to the public), and our team monitors and addresses bugs as needed.

AI Search & Data Strategy

Built to be found — by Google and by AI

Renters are searching differently than they were even a year ago. A growing share of renter and owner searches now happen as conversational questions, bedrooms, pet policy, school district, inside tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google’s AI Overviews, not typed keywords. In that environment, being cited by the AI matters more than ranking on a results page most people never fully see.

The shift

Google’s AI Mode passed one billion monthly users in 2026. Being cited by the AI now matters more than ranking on a page most people never fully read.

The current site already has real material working in its favor: genuine FAQ content, strong local specificity, and real reviews. What is missing is structure, the difference between content existing and content being citable. That is a fast, concrete opportunity, not a rebuild-from-nothing problem.

We start with a Discovery Audit — included in the build, not billed separately

  • Technical SEO and AEO audit covering site structure, schema, and FAQ and content citability.
  • Google Business Profile and directory-consistency review across Google, Yelp, BBB, and other listings, the single biggest lever for AI-search visibility for a local business, ahead of the website itself.
  • Ad-to-application conversion tracking setup, so ad spend can finally be tied to actual applications submitted.

From there, ongoing SEO and AEO and data support is available in tiers, scoped to what the audit actually finds. See Your Investment for the starting structure.

Our Work

Examples of how we accomplish great web design

A few projects that speak to our approach, each with its own unique aspects and needs.

AWeekAway.org↗

A design and development project we just completed for A Week Away, a non-profit we’ve partnered with for the last seven years. The site itself is simple, but we integrated it into their existing donation ecosystem to improve internal processes, and built it around distinct user profiles: families, medical professionals, and caregivers.

VisionCorps.net↗

A large undertaking: essentially two websites for one organization, built to serve two very different audiences, individuals with vision impairment, and the businesses that purchase products from VisionCorps’ manufacturing division. Each needed its own user journeys, information architecture, features, and functionality.

EverestAutographs.com↗

A fun, genuinely unique project. This client came to us because he’d heard we take on work that isn’t cookie-cutter, a niche business selling celebrity autographs and memorabilia to collectors worldwide, striking out on his own after years running the show for one of the biggest players in the space. His new business essentially was the website, so it had to make people feel cared for while spending large sums, from first visit through delivery.

Because of the complexities, celebrity agents, high-profile buyers, and the collectible industry, there were many moving pieces. We built a custom online ordering and inventory system tailored to his needs, integrated seamlessly with the specific payment-verification platform his higher-profile buyers require, and tied directly into his existing protocol for authenticity and tracking on every collectible.

CEPontzsons.com↗

A smaller site for a client who wanted their personality and approach to stand out in a crowded field. In discovery we uncovered a real need: a way for ideal clients to qualify, or disqualify, themselves before reaching out. We built a simple, custom “Pricing Guide” that lets visitors adjust variables to see what kind of project their budget supports, so they can decide for themselves whether to request a quote.

LancasterMennonite.org↗

A large project built to work for many different user groups, over 100 pages, with a range of integrations and features.

HourglassLancaster.com↗

Another smaller site with one demanding requirement: a searchable archive of everything Hourglass curates, creates, and catalogs, organized so it’s easy to navigate and search. We built a custom resource library and flexible filtering system to make it work.

On deck: web projects for Aaron’s Acres and Penn Cinema, two organizations we’ve recently rebranded and now run managed marketing strategies for.
To Summarize

Deliverables & scope

Our deliverables to you

  • In-depth discovery: a series of workshops to better understand your goals for the website and the unique opportunities we can leverage for the build.
  • Discovery Audit: technical SEO and AEO review, Google Business Profile and directory-consistency check, and conversion-tracking setup.
  • Finalized Website Outline (up to 2 rounds of revisions), organized around renter and owner paths.
  • Migration and restructuring of existing listings, including the shift from individual apartment-unit pages to floor-plan pages.
  • Custom theme styling on modern, Gutenberg-based WordPress.
  • Flexible community-page template supporting individual logos and color schemes per property.
  • Homepage design featuring the single-family and multifamily path split and a dynamic, data-driven search module.
  • Native WordPress listings architecture with no third-party listing-plugin dependency.
  • Self-service editing capability, plus a training session for your team.
  • Ad-to-application conversion tracking implementation.
  • Browser and device compatibility testing.
  • 30-day post-launch support to ensure your new site is running smoothly.

What’s out of scope for this proposal

  • New logo or brand-identity design for Wolf & Kline itself. Individual community logos and color schemes, where they already exist, are incorporated as part of the build.
  • Ongoing content creation, blog writing, or site additions beyond the 30-day post-launch window, which is limited to remedial work only.
  • Ongoing SEO, AEO, advertising, and data support beyond the Discovery Audit, which is available separately and detailed below.
Your Investment

Investment

Website Design & DevelopmentInvestment
Website Design & DevelopmentFull build per the process and deliverables above.$31,500

Final investment is confirmed after the Website Outline (page count, listings-migration scope, and number of community templates) is approved.

Managed Hosting & Data Infrastructure — $75–150/mo

Hosting is provided on modern, WordPress-optimized infrastructure, with your media library (150GB and growing) offloaded to cloud object storage rather than stored on the host directly. That is the difference between hosting costs that stay flat and hosting costs that grow with every landlord photo you add.

  • Critical updates: security fixes, theme and plugin updates, WordPress upgrades.
  • Daily cloud-based backup, with restore support included.
  • Offloaded media storage and CDN delivery.
  • SSL certificate.
  • Website monitoring and malware removal support.
  • Dedicated staging environment for troubleshooting and updates.

Ongoing Maintenance & Support — $250–400/mo

Covers the technical upkeep this build’s complexity requires, custom listing types, per-community theming, and a media library that needs ongoing housekeeping, plus dedicated support hours for small changes and design pivots.

AI Search (AEO/SEO) & Data Support

Included in the build is the Discovery Audit described above. Ongoing work is tiered and scoped to the audit’s findings:

TierMonthly
FoundationBaseline technical SEO and AEO hygiene, schema and structure upkeep, and monthly reporting.$400 / mo
GrowthAdds proactive content and blog work targeted at real keyword and AEO gaps.$1,500 / mo
AccelerateAdds advertising optimization support and additional hours.$1,500–2,500+ / mo

Tier selection and final pricing follow the Discovery Audit findings, not a guess made before we have looked.

We’re excited about the possibility of building this with you. Just reply to this proposal and we’ll get the Discovery on the calendar.
